response: The proposed account leaks unavailable future information into the decision: realized weekend demand is a state observed only after the initial allocation, not an input the manager can use when allocating. The research problem is instead an ex ante, single-stage inventory-allocation problem. Before the weekend, the depot chooses a nonnegative allocation to each service zone, subject to the fixed total inventory, using the joint demand distribution and shortage and leftover costs. After demand realizes, each zone experiences a shortage or leftover based on its advance allocation; inventory cannot be transferred or rebalanced. The objective is to minimize expected shortage and leftover cost across possible joint demand outcomes. Treating the joint distribution as representative of weekend demand is a model assumption. The supplied material does not establish that the manager knows realized zone demand in advance or has any post-demand recourse, so both claims must be excluded.
